creator: obrien@FreeBSD.org
text:
On Mon, May 22, 2000 at 06:29:01PM -0500, Ade Lovett wrote:
> The (extremely small) chances of this scenario happening are
> _much_ larger than having mutt somehow decide to use a customized
> libintl.a which is totally incompatible with one that's already
> installed.

I see ache alread applied this PR agaist my will.
The first time I copy a mutt binary to Hub and I cannot use it as it
depends on a non-standard library and I get frustraited, it is out.

 
> I fail to see what harm is being done by the inclusion of
> devel/gettext as a library dependency.  If anything, it make
> things potentially more consistent.

If you guys are going to do this, then do it completely:

    ===>  Configuring for mutt-1.2
    ...
    creating intl/Makefile
    ===>  Building for mutt-1.2
    cc -I/usr/local/include -DSHAREDIR=\"/usr/local/share/mutt\"
    -DSYSCONFDIR=\"/usr/local/etc\"    -DBINDIR=\"/usr/local/bin\"
    -DHAVE_CONFIG_H=1 -I. -I. -I. -I./imap -Iintl -I/usr/local/include
    -I./intl  -Wall -pedantic  -pipe -O -Wall -c makedoc.c
    ...


--- Makefile.in.fbsd    Tue May 23 09:56:50 2000
+++ Makefile.in Tue May 23 10:03:24 2000
@@ -139 +139 @@
-INCLUDES = -I$(top_srcdir) -I. $(IMAP_INCLUDES) -Iintl -I$(includedir)
+INCLUDES = -I$(top_srcdir) -I. $(IMAP_INCLUDES) -I$(includedir)

--- configure.fbsd        Tue May 23 10:09:49 2000
+++ configure     Tue May 23 10:10:34 2000
@@ -6248 +6248 @@
-CPPFLAGS="$CPPFLAGS -I\$(top_srcdir)/intl"
+CPPFLAGS="$CPPFLAGS"

--- imap/Makefile.in.orig       Tue May  9 08:15:52 2000
+++ imap/Makefile.in    Tue May 23 10:12:49 2000
@@ -119 +119 @@
-INCLUDES = -I$(top_srcdir) -I../intl
+INCLUDES = -I$(top_srcdir)


-- 
-- David  (obrien@FreeBSD.org)
